May 2, 2026

Origin: Dong Van, Ha Giang, Vietnam

The Mist Tea: Shan Tuyet — 300-Year-Old Tea Trees Hidden in Vietnam's Cloud Forest

High in the limestone mountains of Ha Giang, where clouds drift through ancient forests at 1,200 to 1,800 meters above sea level, grow tea trees that have stood for over three centuries. This is Shan Tuyet — Vietnam's most distinguished ancient tea variety — and Greenie Vietnam brings it to premium tea retailers, specialty importers, and private label brands worldwide.

Where the last tendrils of morning fog wrap around 300-year-old tea ancestors in the Dong Van karst plateau — at 1,600 meters above the worries of the commodity market — a tea of mythic distinction is born: Trà Shan Tuyết Mây Mù, the Mist Tea of Ha Giang.

The Living Antiquities of Dong Van

Shan Tuyet (literally "snow mountain") is not a plantation tea. These are wild and semi-wild Camellia sinensis var. assamica trees — some reaching heights of 10 meters or more — that have grown naturally in the Hoang Lien Son mountain range for hundreds of years. Unlike cultivated tea bushes that are pruned waist-high for easy harvesting, Shan Tuyet trees must be climbed by skilled ethnic H'Mong and Dao pickers who hand-select only the finest buds and leaves.

Each of these arboreal giants is genetically unique, grown from seed on the same limestone outcrops where their ancestors took root during the Qing dynasty. The root systems penetrate fissures up to 15 meters deep into mineral-rich karst, drawing water filtered through layers of fossil-studded limestone.

The Tree Census

There are fewer than 10,000 mature wild Shan Tuyet trees remaining in Ha Giang. Each tree yields only 3-5kg of finished tea per year — less than 1/10th the yield of a plantation tea bush of equivalent canopy size. This is not a commodity; this is a limited-edition botanical harvest.

Why Shan Tuyet Stands Alone

The science behind Shan Tuyet's extraordinary character is only now being understood through advanced phytochemical analysis.

1. Extreme Altitude Biochemistry

Growing at 1,200–1,800m ASL, Shan Tuyet trees experience cold nights, intense daytime UV radiation, and persistent mountain mist. This stress triggers the production of protective polyphenols — particularly EGCG (epigallocatechin gallate) — at concentrations up to 40% higher than lowland tea varieties. The result is a tea with remarkable fragrance, natural sweetness, and a smooth texture that lacks the bitterness common in lower-altitude teas.

2. Volcanic Terroir

The Dong Van karst plateau is ancient seabed lifted by tectonic forces millions of years ago. The limestone is embedded with marine fossils, and the mineral composition — particularly calcium, magnesium, and trace selenium — creates a tea with a distinct minerality and a deep, resonant hậu vị (lingering aftertaste) that persists for 15-20 minutes after drinking.

3. Pesticide-Free by Nature

At these altitudes, in isolated forest ecosystems, pests are naturally scarce. The trees have never known chemical fertilizers or pesticides. Shan Tuyet is inherently clean — a rare claim that requires no certification gymnastics to justify.

The Living Process

From the moment H'Mong pickers climb these ancient giants at dawn to the final sun-curing on bamboo trays, every step is done by hand.

I. The Climb

Ethnic H'Mong pickers ascend 8-10 meter trees at dawn, selecting only the "1 bud 2 leaves" — the most aromatic and complex stage of growth.

II. The Hand-Rolling

Leaves are withered on bamboo trays in cool mountain air, then hand-rolled by artisans who adjust pressure by feel — an irreproducible skill.

III. The Mountain Cure

Controlled oxidation unfolds in mountain air, followed by gentle sun-drying or traditional firing over longan wood embers.

The Tasting Experience

The Liquor

Golden-amber, translucent, with a slight opacity that signals high polyphenol content. The first steep releases wildflower honey, ripe yellow peach, and a whisper of orchid.

The Evolution

Second steep: dried longan, wood sap. Third steep: minerals emerge — wet limestone, distant smoke. Fourth through seventh: the tea continues to evolve, never fading.

Tree Age: 200–500 years
Altitude: 1,200–1,800m
Steeps: 7+ infusions

Forms Available for Export

Greenie supplies Shan Tuyet in several forms to match your market and customer base:

  • Green Shan Tuyet — Minimally oxidized, preserving the freshest floral and vegetal notes. Ideal for customers who appreciate delicate, fragrant teas.
  • White Shan Tuyet — Only the downy silver buds, withered and sun-dried. The rarest and most precious form, with an ethereal honey sweetness.
  • Black (Red) Shan Tuyet — Fully oxidized, yielding a robust, malty cup with dried fruit and cocoa notes. Excellent for Western tea drinkers and blending.
  • Aged Shan Tuyet — Compressed cakes or loose leaf aged in controlled conditions, developing earthy, medicinal depth over time.

Packaging Options

  • Loose leaf bulk — Vacuum-sealed in food-grade foil bags, 1kg–20kg, for repackers and tea houses.
  • Retail tins — Premium metal tins with your private label artwork.
  • Kraft stand-up pouches — Natural look with degassing valves for freshness.
  • Gift sets — Curated collections combining green, white, and black Shan Tuyet.

The First-Mover Advantage

The global specialty tea market is growing steadily at 8.5% CAGR, and buyers are increasingly looking beyond the familiar origins of China, Japan, and India. Vietnam's tea story is still being written on the world stage, which means early importers of Shan Tuyet have a first-mover advantage. This is not a commodity product — it is a story-rich, premium offering with natural scarcity that commands strong margins for retailers who can tell its story well.

Documentation and Compliance

  • Product specification sheets with full tasting notes
  • Certificate of Analysis (COA) on request
  • SGS or Eurofins testing for pesticide residue, heavy metals, and microbiological safety
  • Country of Origin: Ha Giang, Vietnam
  • Altitude and harvest season documentation

Commercial Terms

  • MOQ: From 1 metric ton (flexible for trial orders)
  • Lead time: 7–15 days depending on form and packaging
  • Incoterms: FOB or CIF from Saigon Port
  • Payment: T/T and L/C based on buyer requirements
  • Samples: Available for buyer evaluation before wholesale orders

Request samples to experience Shan Tuyet firsthand. Greenie Vietnam provides specification sheets, COA documentation, and packaging options so you can evaluate this extraordinary tea with confidence.
